heading: Compensation of Secretaries for Senate Majority and Minority
label: 6151 Compensation of Secretaries for Senate Majority and Minority
text: The Secretary for the Majority of the Senate (other than the incumbent holding office on ) and the Secretary for the Minority of the Senate shall each be paid at an annual rate of compensation of $39,500.April 1, 1977
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 61g of this title

Amendments2019— repealed . See 1975 Amendment note below.Pub. L. 116–94Pub. L. 94–59, § 1051977— substituted “” for “”. Provisions covering the compensation of the incumbent holding the office of Secretary for the Majority of the Senate on , were dropped as executed. See successor provisions set out as a note below.Pub. L. 95–26April 1, 1977July 1, 1975July 1, 19751975—, which increased annual rate of compensation of both Secretary for Majority of Senate and Secretary for Minority of Senate from $38,190 to $39,500 and substituted provisions excepting incumbent Secretary for Majority holding office on , from mandatory payment of $39,500 rate but authorizing payment to him as long as he occupies that position at a maximum annual rate of compensation not to exceed $39,500 for provisions excepting Secretary for Majority holding office on , from mandatory payment of the $38,190 rate but authorizing payment to him as long as he occupied that position at a maximum annual rate of compensation not to exceed $38,190, was repealed by . See above.Pub. L. 94–59Pub. L. 116–94July 1, 1975June 15, 1974

Effective Date of 2019 AmendmentAmendment by effective on the later of the first day of the first applicable pay period beginning on or after , or the first day of the first applicable pay period beginning on or after , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 95–26, title I, § 102(b)91 Stat. 82May 4, 1977“The Majority Leader of the Senate is authorized to fix the compensation of the Secretary for the Majority so long as the position is held by the incumbent holding such position on .”April 1, 1977
